data.title: Japan Capex Outlook Depends on De-Escalation of Mideast Conflict — Market Talk
data.description: If the Middle East conflict shows no signs of de-escalation within the next few months, Japanese companies' appetite for capital investment could deteriorate sharply, says Norinchukin Research Institute economist Takeshi Minami. "While the Takaichi administration, which prioritizes growth, hasn't called for [energy] conservation for now, signs of economic contraction are already appearing in emerging economies where oil reserves are limited," he says. Japan's core machinery orders, considered a leading indicator for capex, increased 13.6% in February from the previous month, government data showed Wednesday. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Asian Equities Rise on Hopes for More U.S.-Iran Talks
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui

Asian equities rose early Wednesday with appetite for risky assets spurred by hopes for more U.S.-Iran talks.

Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran are likely to resume, United Nations Secretary General António Guterres said after meeting with Pakistan's deputy prime minister and foreign minister, Mohammad Ishaq Dar. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. wants to make a deal with Iran where its people can prosper as part of the global economy, but only if Iran commits to not having a nuclear weapon.

Risk-on sentiment was also bolstered after The Wall Street Journal reported, citing two U.S. officials, that more than 20 commercial ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours. The flow of ships is an improvement through the key waterway, which handles one-fifth of the world's oil, although the flow is still a fraction of the levels before the U.S.-Iran conflict.

The U.S. is enforcing a blockade of Iranian ports. Ships that aren't going to Iran's ports aren't subject to the blockade and are being permitted to travel freely.

Markets are "looking past the physical disruption in the Strait [of Hormuz] to the prospect of talks, with risk assets supported," said Taylor Nugent, senior economist at National Australia Bank, in commentary.

Japan's Nikkei Stock Average rose 1.2%, South Korea's Kospi gained 3.1%, and Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index added 0.5%.

Easing concerns over crude supply disruptions in the Middle East weighed on oil prices. Front-month West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures were down 0.9% to $90.42 per barrel, and front-month Brent crude oil futures were 0.4% lower at $94.45 per barrel.

Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com

data.title: Gold steadies near one-week high; eyes on US–Iran talks
data.description:
Gold prices were largely steady on Wednesday, hovering around a one-week high hit earlier in the session, as investors stayed on the sidelines awaiting further clarity on U.S.-Iran peace talks.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold held steady at 4,841.76 per ounce as of 0110 GMT, after hitting its highest level since April 8 earlier in the session.

* U.S. gold futures for June delivery rose 0.3% to $4,866.50.

* "While gold and silver rallied strongly overnight, the broader signal was decisively risk-on rather than defensive positioning," analysts at OCBC said in a note.

* Oil prices fell, and stocks gained, on hopes Iran will resume talks with the U.S. and Israel to end the conflict that has shut the Strait of Hormuz.

* The U.S. dollar hovered near its lowest level in over a month, making dollar-denominated commodities, such as bullion, more affordable for holders of other currencies.

* Talks to end the Iran war could resume in Pakistan over the next two days, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday, after the collapse of weekend negotiations prompted Washington to impose a blockade on Iranian ports.

* Meanwhile, data showed that U.S. producer prices increased less than expected in March as the cost of services was unchanged, but surging energy prices because of the war with Iran were fanning inflation pressures.

* Traders now see a 30% chance of a 25-basis-point U.S. rate cut this year, up from about 13% last week. Before the war, there were expectations of two cuts for this year.

* SPDR Gold Trust , the world's largest gold-backed exchange-traded fund, said its holdings fell 0.5% to 954.48 tonnes on Monday.

* Among other metals, spot silver rose 0.4% to $79.87 per ounce, platinum gained 1.1% to $2,127.45, and palladium was up 0.1% to $1,596.28.

data.title: Australian stocks edge higher as investors focus on US-Iran talks
data.description:
Australian shares rose for a second straight session on Wednesday, with miners leading the rally, as signs that diplomatic engagement may continue between the United States and Iran improved investor sentiment.

The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 0.3% to 8,995.10 by 0025 GMT after a 0.5% gain in the previous session.

While markets nervously track traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, with shipping volumes at a fraction of pre-war levels, the prospect of a second round of U.S.-Iran peace talks has steadied sentiment.

U.S. President Donald Trump and Iranian officials said talks could resume in Pakistan this week, with one source saying backchannel talks have produced progress over some of the sticking points that had caused the last round to fall through.

In Sydney, miners rose 0.8%, helped by a 3.4% gain in gold stocks after bullion prices climbed overnight.

Evolution Mining jumped as much as 8% despite posting a fall in quarterly gold production, while Northern Star Resources (NST) added 2.5%.

BHP rose 0.4% after a Reuters report that China - its largest customer - has lifted bans on procurement of iron ore from the mining giant.

Financials added 0.4%, with Commonwealth Bank of Australia and NAB up 0.3% and 0.8%, respectively. Real estate stocks (.AXRE) gained 0.8%.

Rate-sensitive sectors face an uncertain outlook as policymakers assess how the Iran war is affecting economic growth and inflation.

A top central banker said on Monday he was not confident that interest rates were at the right level to tame inflation.

Energy stocks fell 2.5%, mirroring an overnight fall in crude oil prices.

Among individual stocks, Virgin Australia gained as much as 12.34% after it flagged higher fuel costs, but kept its full-year earnings forecast unchanged.

Mesoblast surged as much as 7% after the drugmaker acquired a worldwide licence to a chimeric antigen receptor platform.

In New Zealand, the benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index rose 0.5% to 13,083.38.

data.title: BULL, HOOD Stocks Pop After-Hours As SEC Clears Major Barrier That Locked Retail Investors Out Of Active Day Trading
data.description:
Key points:
  • The SEC approved sweeping changes removing the long-standing $25,000 pattern day-trader equity requirement.
  • Under the old framework, margin-account holders needed $25,000 in equity to make frequent same-day trades within a five-business-day window.
  • Bernstein maintained an 'Outperform' rating on HOOD, citing a recovery in crypto trading and growth in prediction markets revenue.

Shares of Webull Corp. (BULL) and Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) jumped in extended trading on Tuesday after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) approved sweeping changes to day-trading rules that had limited smaller investors’ ability to trade on margin.

BULL stock jumped 7% in extended trading, while HOOD gained 5% after both stocks had already climbed over 10% in the regular session.

SEC Scraps $25K Day-Trading Rule

Under the old rules, margin account holders who made four or more same-day trades within five business days were required to keep at least $25,000 in their accounts to continue day trading, a threshold that effectively locked out smaller investors from active trading.

The new framework replaces that trade-count limit with intraday margin standards focused on the actual risk in a customer's positions at any given moment during the session. Rather than restricting investors based on the number of trades they make, brokerages will now monitor whether those trades push an account beyond permitted risk levels. Customers may still be required to add funds or reduce positions if their exposure grows too large.

Retail Trading Gets Another Boost From BULL

Under Webull’s prior rules, margin-account users with balances below $25,000 were limited to three day trades within a five-business-day period before triggering the pattern day-trader status. 

Cash accounts were allowed unlimited same-day trades using settled funds, but selling securities purchased with proceeds from a recent sale before those proceeds were officially settled, known as a “good faith violation,” could lead to temporary restrictions on further trading.

The regulatory progress comes as brokerage platforms continue rolling out measures to improve access for retail investors. On Monday, Webull’s Canadian unit introduced zero-commission trading across its platform for U.S. and Canadian equities. The benefit applies across account types, including margin accounts, cash accounts, Tax-Free Savings Accounts and Registered Retirement Savings Plans, and includes access to advanced charting tools, real-time market data and multi-leg options strategies.

“By eliminating commissions, Webull Canada is removing a layer of friction from trading, and delivering on our continued commitment to making capital markets more accessible to Canadian investors,” Webull Canada CEO Michael Constantino said in a statement. 

Robinhood Improves Retail Onboarding 

Robinhood has also been expanding its financial services ecosystem by improving onboarding. On Tuesday, the company selected fintech infrastructure provider Pinwheel as its direct-deposit switching partner for Robinhood Banking, integrating Pinwheel’s PreMatch tech to streamline payroll-linked deposit activation during account opening.

Pinwheel said its PreMatch tech enables near-instant payroll identification and delivers double the conversion rate compared with legacy switching workflows requiring manual payroll-provider searches.

Bernstein Flags ‘Asymmetric Upside’ In HOOD

Separately, Bernstein maintained an Outperform rating on HOOD with a $130 price target, implying a 64% upside from current levels. The firm said the stock offers “asymmetric upside potential,” citing expectations for a recovery in crypto-trading activity and "breakout” growth in prediction-markets revenue. 

Bernstein added that a weaker first-quarter report appears already reflected in the share price and said investors may begin focusing on improving trading volumes from the second quarter onward.

How Do Retail Traders Feel About BULL, HOOD?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment toward HOOD and BULL was ‘extremely bullish’, with message volume marked as ‘high’ for HOOD and ‘extremely high’ for BULL.

One user said, “The question is, what day will this take effect? Traders are ready to deposit 2K And trade all they want. Nobody realizes how big this is until it gets priced in. This is massive for Robinhood.”

https://stocktwits.com/24_7Markets/message/650281558

Another user expects BULL stock to jump 5x from current levels following the cancellation of the $25,000 minimum day-trading requirement.

https://stocktwits.com/tkathlina/message/650279985

Over the past year, HOOD shares have jumped 79%, while BULL stock has plunged 91%.

data.title: Anta Sports' Outperformance Over Peers Likely to Be Sustained — Market Talk
data.description: Anta Sports Products' outperformance versus its peers is likely to be sustained despite macro headwinds, say DBS Group Research's Alison Fok and Mavis Hui in a note. The sports apparel maker's 1Q operating results came in ahead of market expectations, with its Fila brand sales achieving low-teens growth in the quarter, the analysts say. Its main Anta brand sales also performed better than expected, growing by single digits compared with a decline in 4Q. The company noted encouraging signals from Nike's intention to reduce discounting in the China market, which could drive healthier industry discount levels. DBS reiterates its buy rating and maintains its target price of HK$109.00. Shares last closed at HK$84.95.(megan.cheah@wsj.com)
